The attacks involved a combination of ballistic and cruise missiles, with Iranian state media reporting that over 100 missiles and drones were launched. Israeli air defenses, aided by the United States, United Kingdom, France, Jordan, and Bahrain, intercepted the vast majority of the incoming projectiles. Israeli officials reported limited damage and no casualties, although some minor injuries were sustained. The IRGC’s attack was preceded by a public announcement from Iran indicating its intent to retaliate for the Damascus strike. This announcement served as a warning to Israel and the international community, allowing them time to prepare for the response. The timing of the attack, occurring on a Saturday night in Israel, likely aimed to maximize impact and minimize civilian casualties. The Damascus airstrike, attributed by Western media to Israel, significantly escalated tensions between Iran and Israel. The IRGC officers killed were reportedly involved in coordinating Iranian support for Hezbollah and other proxy groups operating in Lebanon and Syria. Iran has consistently sought to expand its influence in the region through these proxy forces, challenging U.S. and Israeli interests. Following the missile attacks, Iran has signaled a willingness to engage in diplomatic efforts to de-escalate the situation, although it has also warned of further action if Israel responds.
